{{Quote|It's all in the clear, as far as the letter of the law goes. But it couldn't be more retrograde to the spirit of the law. Since the asteroid was in Alliance space, and clearly in their jurisdiction, though, there's nothing wrong with what they did, legally speaking.|Gopple|<ref name="HNN48">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|48|business/13321_1.html|Corporate Alliance Attacks Slicer Compound}}</ref>}}
'''Gopple''' was the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of [[Erigorm]] in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] during the onset of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Biography==
Gopple served as [[Erigorm]]'s [[Senator/Legends|senator]] in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]]. Gopple served on the [[Procurement Panel/Legends|Procurement Panel]] of the [[Naval Subcommittee/Legends|Naval Subcommittee]] alongside Senator [[V'troren Tara|V'troren]]. During the Senator's tenure on the Panel, Gopple oversaw the testimony of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Jerjerrod (admiral)|Jerjerrod]] when the latter was summoned to account for public statements that called into question the procurement procedure of the Republic for its naval forces. Jerjerrod maintained that he had not released the statements to the public, but that they had been taken from reports he had filed with Naval Command.<ref name="Testimony" />

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Gopple commented on the legality of the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance's]] [[Raid on Yirt-4138-Grek-12|raid]] on a [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12 slicer compound|compound]] on the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] [[Yirt-4138-Grek-12]]. Despite believing it to be against the spirit of the law, Gopple commented that it was legally permissible as the asteroid fell within the borders of Corporate Alliance space.<ref name="HNN48" />
